subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 2 — 2026-07-09 — One River, Two Ways to Count It: AI vs the Tower
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 2

2026-07-09 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/festive-darwin-c9jczw

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (4.94 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: One River, Two Ways to Count It: AI vs the Tower

2 · Paste the post copy

A hand-clicker on a Bristol Bay tower has counted the sockeye since 1955. This year an AI drone wants that job.

On the Wood River the drone launches itself at 8:30 a.m. and films the run below. A computer-vision model is being trained to count the fish, but it is not trained yet.

The tower crews still produce every count the fleet actually lives by, clicking each passing fish by hand. Fish and Game, which runs the towers, is not part of the pilot. The machine is measured against them, not run with them.

The deck is 9 slides on who decides when Bristol Bay can fish, the 70-year tower method against a first-season drone, and a scorecard where the machine's accuracy cell is still blank.

Would you open or close a fishery on a count the machine has not yet proven?

#BristolBay #FisheriesScience #AppliedAI

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources
KDLG, AI drones and salmon, what new technology could mean for Bristol Bay sockeye counts (Jessie Sheldon, July 3, 2026)

Report card

CriterionScoreWeightNotes
Thumb-stop cover80.1411-word 3-line headline with 'count' in sockeye-red, single focal fish wearing both marks, visibly Bristol Bay; reads at thumb; sockeye silhouette sli
Story arc & swipe momentum80.14Clean beats hook/stakes/old-way/machine/3D-breather/scorecard/who-counts/why-now/close; slide 2 pays off instantly; timeline spine + evolving glyph read as one
Artwork craft & genuine detail70.16Varied instruments (tally field, detection frame, audit table, constellation, convergence node, real-3D riverbank), but several slides carry large empty lower t
Story-art fusion90.14Standout: bar fill = 61.1%, 144 tally columns = 24h/10min, fewer boxes than fish = in training, 70:1 years bar, dashed ADF&G exclusion, five converging tech
Alaska authenticity & local relevance90.12Named throughout: Bristol Bay, Wood River, ADF&G since 1955, UW FRI, BBRSDA, BBEDC, Ian Chiu, Norm Van Vactor, real tower numbers, coords near Aleknagik.
Insight density & factual weight80.1Every content slide adds new verified specifics (44.05M run, 26,944,183 counted, 2,112,834 tower, 57,258 Sunday, 9 sites, quote, partner topology); ~38-45 words
Variety vs ledger80.08Diverges from the only prior deck on all six axes (hero, atmosphere, continuity, hook, palette, type) with justified dials; capped because divergence from a sin
Legibility & platform fitness70.07Body 32-34px, headlines large and clear; vector PDF 4.94MB with title and counters; but 43 sub-24px decorative mono labels strain at 432px thumb.
Copy: voice & hook80.05Hook lands in 111 chars, 815 total, analytical position-taking, deck-summary line present, real debatable close, grade 8-10.
Deliverable completeness80.1copy.json complete: title, exactly 3 hashtags, first-comment source block with three resolving URLs, deck summary, closing question; PDF vector 9 slides; image

Editor's note

No hard fails. Best-in-class story-art fusion and an honest UNPROVEN/in-training thesis on every slide. Before posting, confirm the inline previews load and that the image and PDF URLs resolve (verified 200 on main at ship time).

Automation changes this run (1)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
assets		New committed helper assets/js/aktype.js: AK.fitText(el, {min, max, maxLines}) binary-searches a display headline's font-size until it renders in maxLines line boxes with no overflow (clamps to min + sets data-fit-overflow=1 if the box is genuinely too small), so a large headline can never silently soft-wrap an extra line into the block below it; wired into DESIGN_DOCTRINE section 2 and SKILL.md as the one fit-to-box implementation for future decks.
why: Run 2026-07-09 recurring #1 defect: slides 1, 4, 7, 8 each set a large Archivo headline in a container narrower than its longest line; each soft-wrapped an extra line and machine_qa FAILED it as a tex		revert the upgrade(2026-07-09) commit
upgrade(2026-07-09) commit on branch tsturg/festive-darwin-c9jczw (revert this single prefixed commit to roll back)

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tuesday to Thursday, 8 to 11am Alaska time; document posts perform best midweek.
  • Paste the sources block as the FIRST comment within 60 seconds of posting.
  • Reply substantively to every comment in the first 60 to 90 minutes; threaded author replies drive reach.
  • Note saves and comments at the 48-hour mark and log anything worth an instinct for next run.
  • Confirm the inline slide previews load (they pull from raw.githubusercontent on main); every image URL and the PDF URL were verified to resolve (HTTP 200).
